meta.name: Shawarma Rice
meta.subtitle: Golden, aromatic rice to serve with chicken, beef, or lamb shawarma

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed as a shawarma-platter base: warm spice, subtle tang, and fluffy grains.
Works with basmati or jasmine; includes optional toasted vermicelli like many Middle Eastern rice styles.

summary: Fluffy golden rice seasoned with shawarma spices, turmeric, and aromatics. A perfect base for shawarma bowls and platters, with optional toasted vermicelli and a bright lemon finish.

optional.label: Rice cooker
optional.notes: If using a rice cooker, sauté aromatics/spices in a pan first, then add to cooker with rinsed rice and liquid.

notes: If using vermicelli, choose gluten-free vermicelli to keep gluten-free.
If using butter/ghee, not dairy-free. Olive oil keeps it dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Basmati rice (or long-grain jasmine)
ingredients.notes: Rinse until water runs mostly clear for fluffy grains.

ingredients.label: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Or use ghee/butter for richer flavor (not dairy-free).

ingredients.label: Butter or ghee
ingredients.notes: Optional; swap with 1 tbsp more olive oil.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste; reduce if serving with salty meats/sauces.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock or vegetable stock (or water)
ingredients.notes: Stock boosts flavor; water is fine.

ingredients.label: Vermicelli noodles, broken (optional)
ingredients.notes: Classic Middle Eastern rice add-in. Omit to keep gluten-free.

ingredients.label: Toasted sliced almonds (or pine nuts)
ingredients.notes: Adds crunch; keep nut-free if needed.

steps.title: Rinse and drain the rice
instructions: Rinse rice under cool water until it runs mostly clear (30–60 seconds).
Drain well in a fine-mesh sieve.

steps.title: Sauté aromatics and toast spices
instructions: Heat olive oil (and butter/ghee if using) in a pot over medium heat.
Add onion and cook 3–5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in shawarma spice blend, turmeric, and optional cumin/coriander; toast 20–30 seconds.

steps.title: Toast vermicelli (optional)
instructions: If using vermicelli, add it to the pot and stir constantly 1–2 minutes until lightly golden.

steps.title: Coat and toast the rice
instructions: Add drained rice to the pot.
Stir 60–90 seconds so grains are coated with the spiced oil and lightly toasted.
checks: Rice looks glossy and evenly coated

steps.title: Add liquid and simmer
instructions: Add stock (or water), salt, pepper, and bay leaf (optional).
Bring to a gentle boil, then immediately reduce heat to low.
Cover tightly and simmer 15 minutes without lifting the lid.

steps.title: Rest and fluff
instructions: Turn off heat and let the rice rest, covered, for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Optional: stir in lemon juice for brightness.
Top with parsley and optional toasted almonds/pine nuts.
Serve with shawarma meats, toum, tahini sauce, pickles, and salad.

fridge.notes: Cool quickly and store airtight. Reheat with a splash of water; cover to steam.

freezer.notes: Freeze in flat portions. Reheat covered with a splash of water to restore fluffiness.

batchNotes: Double the recipe for meal prep; rice is a strong bowl base for 3–4 days.
Keep lemon juice separate if freezing; add after reheating.

notes: If you do not have shawarma spice blend, use: 1 tsp cumin + 1 tsp coriander + 1 tsp paprika + 1/2 tsp allspice + pinch cinnamon.
Stock can be swapped for water; add a bit more salt if using water.
For extra restaurant flavor, bloom the spices in fat and toast the rice briefly—don’t skip those steps.
If your rice cooker struggles with aromatics, sauté aromatics/spices first, then add to cooker.
For a richer platter, stir in 1–2 tbsp minced toasted onions or a spoon of pan drippings from shawarma meat.
Sauté onion/garlic/spices in a pan, then add to rice cooker with rinsed rice and liquid.
Cook on standard white rice setting; rest 10 minutes before fluffing.
Increase turmeric to 1 1/2 tsp and add 1 tsp paprika.
Finish with a tiny pinch of saffron if available.
Omit vermicelli or use gluten-free vermicelli.
